TH.label: TH (voiceless)
TH.tip: Place the tip of your tongue lightly between your teeth and blow air through. No voice — just air.

DH.label: TH (voiced)
DH.tip: Place the tip of your tongue lightly between your teeth and add your voice. The sound should buzz slightly.

R.label: R
R.tip: Curl the tip of your tongue back slightly without touching the roof of your mouth. Round your lips a little.

L.label: L
L.tip: Touch the tip of your tongue to the ridge just behind your top teeth. Keep your mouth open.

V.label: V
V.tip: Press your top teeth lightly against your bottom lip and add your voice. The lip should vibrate.

W.label: W
W.tip: Round your lips into a small circle, then quickly relax them. Your teeth should not touch your lip.

F.label: F
F.tip: Press your top teeth lightly against your bottom lip and blow air through. No voice.

B.label: B
B.tip: Press your lips together, build up a small amount of air, then release with voice.

P.label: P
P.tip: Press your lips together, build up air, then release sharply. No voice.

T.label: T
T.tip: Touch the tip of your tongue to the ridge behind your top teeth, then release. No voice.

D.label: D
D.tip: Touch the tip of your tongue to the ridge behind your top teeth, then release with voice.

K.label: K
K.tip: Lift the back of your tongue to the roof of your mouth, build up air, then release. No voice.

G.label: G
G.tip: Lift the back of your tongue to the roof of your mouth, then release with voice.

S.label: S
S.tip: Place your tongue near (not touching) the ridge behind your top teeth and let air hiss through. No voice.

Z.label: Z
Z.tip: Same tongue position as S, but add your voice. The sound should buzz.

SH.label: SH
SH.tip: Round your lips slightly and push air through a wide gap in the middle of your tongue.

ZH.label: ZH
ZH.tip: Same as SH but with your voice. The sound should buzz softly.

CH.label: CH
CH.tip: Start with the tongue near the roof of your mouth (like T), then release with a 'shh'. No voice.

JH.label: J
JH.tip: Same as CH but with your voice. The sound should buzz.

M.label: M
M.tip: Press your lips together and let the sound come out through your nose with voice.

N.label: N
N.tip: Touch the tip of your tongue to the ridge behind your top teeth and let the sound come out through your nose.

NG.label: NG
NG.tip: Lift the back of your tongue to the roof of your mouth and let the sound come out through your nose. No release at the end.

HH.label: H
HH.tip: Open your mouth in the shape of the next vowel and breathe out gently. No voice.

Y.label: Y
Y.tip: Start with your tongue close to the roof of your mouth (like for 'ee') and quickly move into the next vowel.

IY.label: EE (long)
IY.tip: Spread your lips into a wide smile. Hold the sound long. Like 'ee' in see.

IH.label: i (short)
IH.tip: Make a short, relaxed sound with your mouth slightly open. Do not smile widely. Like 'i' in sit.

EH.label: e
EH.tip: Open your mouth a little, with your tongue in the middle. Short. Like 'e' in red.

AE.label: a (cat)
AE.tip: Open your mouth wide and drop your jaw. Short. Like 'a' in cat.

AH.label: u (cup)
AH.tip: Mouth slightly open and relaxed, tongue in the middle. Short and neutral. Like 'u' in cup.

AA.label: ah
AA.tip: Open your mouth wide and drop your jaw fully. Long. Like 'a' in father.

AO.label: aw
AO.tip: Round your lips slightly and lower your jaw. Long. Like 'aw' in saw.

UH.label: u (book)
UH.tip: Round your lips a little. Tongue is back. Short. Like 'oo' in book.

UW.label: oo (long)
UW.tip: Round your lips into a small circle. Hold the sound long. Like 'oo' in food.

OW.label: oh
OW.tip: Start with lips slightly rounded, then close them more. Like 'oh' in go.

EY.label: ay
EY.tip: Start with the mouth open, then close towards a smile. Like 'ay' in say.

AY.label: i (eye)
AY.tip: Start with mouth wide open, then close towards a smile. Like 'i' in time.

AW.label: ow
AW.tip: Start with mouth wide open, then round the lips. Like 'ow' in now.

OY.label: oy
OY.tip: Start with rounded lips, then move to a smile. Like 'oy' in toy.

ER.label: er
ER.tip: Curl your tongue back slightly and make a low 'r'-coloured sound. Like 'er' in her.
